messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Who is the traditionally attributed author of Meditations?

The Meditations is traditionally attributed to Marcus Aurelius, the Roman emperor and Stoic philosopher who reigned from 161 to 180 CE. The work is a collection of personal writings and reflections he composed, likely for his own self-improvement rather than for publication. The attribution to Marcus Aurelius is considered well-established, though the title 'Meditations' was not his own — the original Greek title was something closer to 'To Himself.' It is important not to attribute this work to Plato, Socrates, or Epictetus, despite their prominence in ancient philosophy.
